sections.heading: Systemic
sections.content: Systemic therapy is a cornerstone of ovarian cancer treatment, used in adjuvant, neoadjuvant, and recurrent settings. Platinum-based chemotherapy is standard. Targeted therapies (PARP inhibitors, bevacizumab) and immunotherapies are used based on biomarkers. Treatment is tailored by stage, histology, and molecular profile.

cards.name: Carboplatin/Paclitaxel every 3 weeks
cards.detail: Setting: Primary therapy for stage I-IV disease. Drugs: Paclitaxel 175 mg/m2 IV day 1, Carboplatin AUC 5-6 IV day 1. Cycle days: Repeat every 21 days for 3-6 cycles. Key toxicities: Myelosuppression, neuropathy, alopecia, nausea, fatigue, hypersensitivity reactions (platinum).

cards.name: GOG-218 regimen (Carboplatin/Paclitaxel + Bevacizumab)
cards.detail: Setting: Primary therapy for stage II-IV disease. Drugs: Paclitaxel 175 mg/m2 IV day 1, Carboplatin AUC 6 IV day 1, Bevacizumab 15 mg/kg IV day 1 (starting cycle 2). Cycle days: Repeat every 21 days for 6 cycles; continue bevacizumab for up to 22 cycles total. Key toxicities: Hypertension, proteinuria, hemorrhage, GI perforation, wound healing complications.

cards.name: ICON-7 regimen (Carboplatin/Paclitaxel + Bevacizumab)
cards.detail: Setting: Primary therapy for stage II-IV disease. Drugs: Paclitaxel 175 mg/m2 IV day 1, Carboplatin AUC 5-6 IV day 1, Bevacizumab 7.5 mg/kg IV day 1. Cycle days: Repeat every 21 days for 6 cycles; continue bevacizumab for up to 12 additional cycles. Key toxicities: Similar to GOG-218.

cards.name: Carboplatin/Gemcitabine + Bevacizumab (OCEANS)
cards.detail: Setting: Recurrent platinum-sensitive disease. Drugs: Gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 IV day 1 and 8, Carboplatin AUC 4 IV day 1, Bevacizumab 15 mg/kg IV day 1. Cycle days: Repeat every 21 days. Key toxicities: Myelosuppression, nausea, fatigue, hypertension, proteinuria.

timeline.title: 1st Line
timeline.body: Adjuvant therapy for stage I disease (high-grade serous, endometrioid grade 2/3): Carboplatin/paclitaxel every 3 weeks for 3-6 cycles. Adjuvant therapy for stage II-IV disease: Carboplatin/paclitaxel ± bevacizumab (GOG-218/ICON-7 regimen) every 3 weeks for 6 cycles. Neoadjuvant therapy for advanced disease: Carboplatin/paclitaxel ± bevacizumab for 3-4 cycles.

timeline.title: Maintenance
timeline.body: Based on BRCA/HRD status and prior bevacizumab use. For BRCA-mutated: olaparib, niraparib, or rucaparib. For BRCA wild-type/HRD-positive: olaparib+bevacizumab or niraparib+bevacizumab if bevacizumab used during primary therapy.

timeline.title: 2nd Line
timeline.body: Recurrent platinum-sensitive disease: Combination platinum-based chemotherapy ± bevacizumab (e.g., carboplatin/gemcitabine ± bevacizumab).

timeline.title: 3rd Line
timeline.body: Recurrent platinum-resistant disease: Non-platinum-based chemotherapy ± bevacizumab (e.g., weekly paclitaxel ± bevacizumab, liposomal doxorubicin ± bevacizumab) or mirvetuximab soravtansine-gynx for FRα-expressing tumors.
